label: VictoriaMetrics
description: Connect to a VictoriaMetrics instance to query high-performance Prometheus-compatible metrics and logs.

form.title: Connect VictoriaMetrics
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your VictoriaMetrics endpoint and provide credentials if access is restricted.

methods.label: URL + Bearer Token
methods.description: Paste your VictoriaMetrics base URL and an optional bearer token for authentication. The simplest path for most self-hosted and managed deployments.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with URL and Bearer Token

Most VictoriaMetrics deployments expose a plain HTTP(S) endpoint. If your instance has authentication enabled via a reverse proxy or VictoriaMetrics Enterprise's built-in auth, you'll also need a bearer token.

### Steps

1. Find your VictoriaMetrics base URL. For a self-hosted instance it is typically `http://<host>:8428` (single-node) or `http://<vmselect-host>:8481/select/<accountID>/prometheus` (cluster).
2. Verify the endpoint is reachable: open `<base-url>/health` in a browser — it should return `OK`.
3. If authentication is required (e.g. you fronted VictoriaMetrics with a proxy like vmauth or nginx), obtain or generate a bearer token from that proxy's configuration.
4. Paste the base URL into **Instance URL** below.
5. Paste the token (if any) into **Bearer Token** below.
6. If no auth is required, leave **Bearer Token** blank.

### Notes

- For VictoriaMetrics Cloud (managed), copy the **Endpoint URL** from the Cloud console and use the API token shown there as the bearer token.
- Cluster deployments: use the vmselect URL with your account ID embedded, not the raw vmstorage address.
- The token is stored locally in Anton's encrypted vault and is never sent to Anton's servers.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Instance URL
fields.description: Base URL of your VictoriaMetrics instance, e.g. http://vm-host:8428 or https://your-cluster/select/0/prometheus.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer Token
fields.description: Optional. Sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>. Leave blank if your instance has no authentication.

methods.label: URL + Basic Auth
methods.description: Connect using a username and password — useful when VictoriaMetrics is protected by HTTP basic authentication via a reverse proxy such as nginx or vmauth.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with URL and Basic Auth

If your VictoriaMetrics endpoint is protected by HTTP basic authentication (commonly configured in nginx, Caddy, or VictoriaMetrics Enterprise's vmauth), supply the credentials here.

### Steps

1. Find your VictoriaMetrics base URL (e.g. `https://metrics.example.com`).
2. Confirm basic auth is enabled by running:
   `curl -u user:pass https://metrics.example.com/health`
   It should return `OK`.
3. Enter the base URL in **Instance URL** below.
4. Enter the **Username** and **Password** that your reverse proxy or vmauth config requires.

### Notes

- vmauth configuration: credentials are defined in the `users` section of `vmauth.yml`. Check with your infrastructure team if you don't know the credentials.
- Do not use this method for VictoriaMetrics Cloud — use **URL + Bearer Token** with the Cloud-issued API token instead.
- Credentials are stored locally in Anton's encrypted vault.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Instance URL
fields.description: Base URL of your VictoriaMetrics instance protected by basic authentication.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: HTTP basic auth username configured in your reverse proxy or vmauth.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: HTTP basic auth password. Stored encrypted in Anton's local vault.

methods.label: URL Only (No Auth)
methods.description: Connect to an unauthenticated VictoriaMetrics instance — suitable for local development or internal network deployments with no access controls.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to an Open VictoriaMetrics Instance

For local development setups or instances that are intentionally open (e.g. running on localhost or behind a VPN with no additional auth layer).

### Steps

1. Start VictoriaMetrics locally or note the host/port of your internal instance.
2. Verify it is reachable: open `http://localhost:8428/health` — it should return `OK`.
3. Paste the base URL into **Instance URL** below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Avoid exposing an unauthenticated VictoriaMetrics instance to the public internet. Use a bearer token, basic auth, or a firewall rule to restrict access.
- For production use, switch to the **URL + Bearer Token** or **URL + Basic Auth** methods.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Instance URL
fields.description: Base URL of your open VictoriaMetrics instance. Typically http://localhost:8428 for local installs.
